title: ranunculus laetus
intro: Ranunculus laetus, also known as the meadow crowfoot, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Ranunculaceae. It is primarily found in wet meadows and along streams, and is native to Europe and parts of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of saponins and alkaloids. Herbal preparations of Ranunculus laetus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
